caption: Two days in Tokyo: After a couple amazing trips to Japan in the last two years, here’s how I would spend two days in Tokyo (one day was not possible) as someone who loves good food, design, and exploring local neighbourhoods 🇯🇵
transcript: If I had two days in Tokyo, here's how I'd spend them. As someone who loves design, good food, and not always being in the busiest areas, here's my guide. I would sit at the Ryoka and Uendai, Betty, and Shimoku to Zawa. Nice, quiet neighborhood, 10 minutes from Shibuya. We would start our first day at Tsukiji Fish Market. I didn't feel the need to get there at 5am, so I got there around 8.30, tried a bunch of food from the stalls, it was fun, it was great. There, we're heading up to Kapabashi to shop around for kitchenware and ceramics. Before heading to Kiroguchi for an omakase lunch. One of my pro tips is to have a lunch omakase rather than dinner, because it's far less expensive, but equally delicious. There, we're gonna wander around Omotosando and Aoyama, shop around. There's so many cool brands in this area, both local Japanese brands and global brands. If you're anything like me, you're gonna need another snack, so we're heading down to Harajuku Gyozaru in Harajuku, grabbing a matcha right after. I'm not a super matcha snob, so I like the matcha Tokyo, and their ice cream is also delicious. And now next is probably your highlight for the day. Up when sunset is in Tokyo while you're there, head to City View Tokyo for 360 sunset view. Put yourself half an hour before and after sunset to take it all in. Dinner, my pro tip would be to ask locals or your hotel where their favorite izakaya is. Menus are all slightly different, but the food is just so delicious. And then finish your first night with a nightcap at Grandfather's SG Club or Tokyo Vinyl Bar. For our next morning, we're gonna have a nice, relaxing, slow morning. We're gonna head to the onsen before having our Japanese breakfast at our hotel. I forgot to mention that our hotel is an onsen, which is the best thing ever. From there, we're gonna grab a coffee at Light Up Coffee, Coffee Culture House, or Nardoi Onokat before heading in to check out the Meiji Jingu Shrine. The grounds in this area are so sprawling and so beautiful. The park, you can just wander through and on. I really love sitting and taking in all the different people that we're wandering through. You can use this as your breath of fresh air for the day. Wander through the Yuyugi Park. Next, you are having the Dombari Bowl to change your life. We're heading to Mukai for this insane uni, tuna, and fish roe bowl. Next, we're heading back to my favorite neighborhood, Shimokutozawa, to shop around. There's so many cool vintage shops, ceramic shops. I can honestly just stumble into them all. I would say this is like the coolest, most trendy neighborhood that I found, but not in an overrun way. Next, we're heading down to another one of my favorite areas, Daikanyama and Nakamaguro. If you want to see cute dogs, fashionable people, and great shops, this is for you. Nearby, you can also head to the La Mer store in Ibisu. It is located in the most beautiful house, so you have to go see it. The stores I really like are Cover Cord 1LDK. Personally, I loved really checking out the store designs and the design in general in this area. If you need an afternoon pick-me-up, head to Onibus for coffee and a banana bread. Personally, I really love heading to these more neighborhood areas because I found that I could get the vibe of the city more. If you want a cultural moment, head to Kyu, Asakura House. It's a really beautiful traditional Japanese home. Another thing that's really famous in Tokyo is the pizza. I really like Sabasu. It's in this really cool, chic house. But you can also head back to Shimokutozawa and just wander around the local restaurants with really cool young people. We stumbled upon this late-night dim sum place that was just insane. And then to finish your night, I think you had to Shinjuku to see the lights and the crowds. It's really fun. You've had a perfect two days in Tokyo. Ciao!
